title: Unified governed memory and data substrate plan defined
objective: Place every LifeOS-native data class under one governed data contract: the hot, warm, and cold memory tiers plus the relational, vector, graph, embedded-ledger, artifact, identity, and provenance content classes (and, for completeness, the per-agent cognition and external-secret classes the blueprint and STORE-001 also enumerate). Each data class receives exactly one owner, one schema, one retention tier, one encryption boundary, one migration path, one backup source, one replay rule, one rollback rule, and one proof task, with no private data exposed. Every assignment is derived from the Architecture Blueprint's intended tiered memory hierarchy and the STORE-001 keystone decision, not invented: PostgreSQL + RuVector is the canonical durable owner of relational and semantic macro-state; redb is the transient high-frequency tier; AgentDB (.rvf) owns per-agent cognition; envctl is the forward-only bridge and projection engine; the OS keyring owns secrets. No existing durable use is silently reclassified.
inputs: generated/task_graph.source.csv (INTEL-001 row: goal, verification_gate, inputs=LIFEOS-005; Master Plan memory model; preserved RuVector, Postgres, redb, graph, and vault capabilities)
1.0_VISION/Notebooklm/Architecture Blueprint - LifeOS Core Foundation.md (tiered memory hierarchy: redb transient vs PostgreSQL+RuVector canonical)
1.0_VISION/Notebooklm/Architecture Context Matrix_ PostgreSQL-First LifeOS Framework - Table 1.csv (canonical-vs-derived state, identity keys, schema fields per component)
generated/adopted_capabilities.source.csv (ADOPT-001/ADOPT-002 BlobStore, PgStore, SHA-256 identity, secrets-engine)

ownership_axes.note: The governed data contract spans two orthogonal axes so that 'one owner per data class' holds without contradiction. A tier class is a physical storage stratum owned by one engine; a content class is a logical entity owned by one authority and physically resident across named tiers. Content ownership is never split across engines; tier residency is placement, not ownership. Example: the relational content class is owned solely by PostgreSQL and is resident in the warm and cold tiers; it is never hot-durable and never dual-written.

work_items.owner: redb (per edge node / process) as the intended transient high-frequency tier
work_items.schema: Key-Value: BLAKE3 content hashes for the dedup cache, agent-local / process-local keys for scratchpad state, monotonic sequence + idempotency key for the outbox, raw byte values (Architecture Context Matrix redb row: 'Exact Key (BLAKE3 Hash for cache) and Raw Byte Values').
work_items.retention_tier: hot: microsecond latency, process-lifecycle / local-disk persistence (blueprint tiered table 'Process Life-cycle / Local Disk'); transient by design.
work_items.encryption_boundary: Local-disk file permissions on the memory-mapped redb file; single-node isolation (no cross-node exposure); never holds secrets (STORE-001 class 3/6: 'redb never holds secrets').
work_items.migration_path: None required. This is redb's intended terminal role (STORE-001 assignment 3 migration_path). Settled/significant state is flushed forward to PostgreSQL by envctl; the tier itself does not migrate.
work_items.backup: None durable by design; redb ACID+MVCC on local disk survives process restart within the lifecycle; on loss, state is transient by definition (STORE-001 class 3 recovery_source).
work_items.replay: Application-level WAL/outbox replays unacknowledged entries forward to PostgreSQL after a PostgreSQL latency spike or outage (blueprint: redb 'safely acts as an application-level Write-Ahead Log'); redb crash-replay via its own ACID transactions.
work_items.rollback: Discard: transient state is dropped, or a redb transaction aborts. No durable-truth rollback obligation, because redb is never the durable authority for a PostgreSQL-owned class.
work_items.proof_task: PGAUTH-002 (redb use-case and authority contract bounded) + POSTGRES-006 (redb and AgentDB runtime boundaries defined).
work_items.decision: redb owns the hot tier as its intended transient role; it never becomes a second durable writer for a PostgreSQL-owned class (STORE-001 assignment 3; forward-only invariant).

work_items.owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector (the live, continuously-queried ACID working set) as single tier-owner engine.
work_items.schema: Relational tables plus RuVector vector/graph columns; standard SQL identities (e.g., user_id, category) alongside semantic_embedding vector columns (Architecture Context Matrix PostgreSQL row).
work_items.retention_tier: warm: millisecond latency, enterprise ACID, active hybrid-search working set (blueprint tiered table 'Enterprise ACID / PITR', 'Global Memory / Relational Analytics').
work_items.encryption_boundary: At-rest database encryption plus role-based SQL permissions; stores references and provenance, never raw secret values.
work_items.migration_path: Fed forward from the hot tier: redb -> envctl(embed) -> PostgreSQL, forward-only; durable classes leave redb-durable only via a verified BlobStore SHA-256 parity cutover (STORE-001 invariant 'Verified cutover').
work_items.backup: PostgreSQL PITR (base backups + continuous WAL archiving).
work_items.replay: WAL replay to a point in time; reproducible re-embedding of semantic indexes from source rows (STORE-001 class 1/2 recovery).
work_items.rollback: Copy-On-Write branch rollback inside the database (blueprint COW branching) and PITR to a chosen timestamp.
work_items.proof_task: PGAUTH-001 (durable macro-state authority contract bounded) + POSTGRES-002 (whole user-space relational graph and identity model) + POSTGRES-005 (RuVector embedding and semantic database editing).
work_items.decision: PostgreSQL + RuVector owns the warm active tier as the intended canonical durable owner; RuVector runs as a native No-Sidecar PostgreSQL extension (STORE-001 assignments 1/2; No-Sidecar invariant).

work_items.owner: PostgreSQL PITR archive (base backups + WAL archive segments + compressed histories flushed by envctl) as single tier-owner substrate.
work_items.schema: Same relational + vector schema, held as archival partitions and compressed-history rows (blueprint: envctl 'flushes settled execution trees and compressed histories into the global PostgreSQL memory').
work_items.retention_tier: cold: long-term archival within the PITR recovery window; forward-only from warm, never reverse.
work_items.encryption_boundary: Encrypted archive at rest; same role boundary as warm; provenance references only, no raw secrets.
work_items.migration_path: Forward archival tiering within PostgreSQL from the warm working set; no reverse path and no separate cold store.
work_items.backup: The cold tier IS the backup substrate: PITR base backups plus archived WAL segments.
work_items.replay: PITR restore to any timestamp inside the retention window via base-backup restore + WAL segment replay.
work_items.rollback: Restore to a prior base backup and replay WAL to the chosen recovery point.
work_items.proof_task: POSTGRES-010 (whole user-space parity recovery and authority cutover defined).
work_items.decision: PostgreSQL PITR archive owns the cold tier; enterprise ACID/PITR is the blueprint-intended persistence semantics for global memory (blueprint tiered table).

work_items.surface: Relational content class (identities-as-rows, histories, permissions, module_path metadata, code AST/rows, blob references)
work_items.schema: Stable PostgreSQL primary identities; history/temporal relations; permission rows; module_path metadata; AST/code rows; SHA-256 blob references (STORE-001 class 1; POSTGRES-002 relational graph and identity model).
work_items.retention_tier: Resident warm (active) and cold (archived); never hot-durable.
work_items.encryption_boundary: At-rest encryption plus role-based row/column permissions; permissions themselves are governed relational rows, not out-of-band state.
work_items.migration_path: redb -> envctl -> PostgreSQL forward-only; CodeDB blob references cut over from redb-durable to PostgreSQL-canonical via the codedb_store_pg::PgStore BlobStore contract, one verified SHA-256 swap at a time (STORE-001 class 1 migration_path).
work_items.replay: WAL replay to a point in time.
work_items.decision: PostgreSQL owns the relational class exactly once; redb holds only a transient forward buffer; envctl is the writer bridge (STORE-001 assignment 1).

work_items.surface: Vector / semantic content class (embeddings, hybrid-search indexes)
work_items.owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector (native No-Sidecar extension) — single canonical owner.
work_items.schema: semantic_embedding vector columns and hybrid-search indexes; all-MiniLM-L6-v2 embeddings generated by a local model during the envctl bridge, linked to source rows and to the BLAKE3 dedup hash (blueprint: 'the database itself does not generate embeddings; it only stores and measures them').
work_items.retention_tier: Warm (active hybrid search) + cold (archived); redb holds only a disposable BLAKE3 dedup cache (hot, non-authoritative).
work_items.encryption_boundary: At-rest; embeddings are derived from source rows and carry no independent secret content.
work_items.migration_path: RuVector-in-PostgreSQL extension activation must be proven locally before full cutover; until then embeddings are produced via the proven Bun-loaded ruvector N-API path and committed to PostgreSQL (STORE-001 class 2 migration_path).
work_items.backup: PostgreSQL PITR plus reproducible re-embedding from source rows (STORE-001 class 2 recovery).
work_items.replay: Deterministic re-embedding from the versioned source rows (embeddings are a reproducible derivation, not primary state).
work_items.rollback: COW branch rollback; re-embed from the prior source revision.
work_items.proof_task: POSTGRES-005 (RuVector embedding and semantic database editing) + NBVERIFY-001 (Bun/ruvector N-API embedding path truth-tested).
work_items.tier_residency: warm + cold (canonical); hot (disposable dedup cache only)
work_items.decision: PostgreSQL + RuVector owns the vector class exactly once with no separate vector sidecar; redb performs f32 distance math only as a cache (STORE-001 assignment 2; No-Sidecar invariant).

work_items.surface: Graph / causal content class (causal dependency edges, RuVector GNN knowledge graph)
work_items.owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector (GNN causal edges inside the database) — single canonical owner.
work_items.schema: Causal dependency edges and GNN nodes/edges whose identity is bound to their source-row identity; MinCut operates over this graph as the swarm immune system (blueprint sections 5-6; STORE-001 class 1 causal edges).
work_items.retention_tier: Warm + cold; MinCut and Causal Graph Verification run live over the graph.
work_items.encryption_boundary: At-rest; graph edges are derived from source rows and carry no independent secret content.
work_items.migration_path: Reproducibly derived from versioned source rows; never an independent authority (POSTGRES-001-A: 'RuVector vector/graph indexes are reproducibly derived from versioned source rows and are never an independent authority').
work_items.backup: PostgreSQL PITR plus reproducible re-derivation from relational source.
work_items.replay: Re-derive causal edges from the relational source; re-run Causal Graph Verification, which rejects any edge lacking a historical root.
work_items.rollback: COW branch rollback; re-derive the graph from the prior source revision.
work_items.proof_task: POSTGRES-002 (graph edges in the relational model) + POSTGRES-005 (MinCut isolation and causal graph).
work_items.decision: PostgreSQL + RuVector owns the runtime knowledge graph as a derived, single-authority class inside the No-Sidecar database (STORE-001 assignment 2; blueprint Causal Graph Verification).

work_items.surface: Embedded-ledger content class (Cryptographic Witness Chains + append-only proof ledger)
work_items.owner: PostgreSQL anchors the durable witness-chain roots and the database-native proof ledger; AgentDB holds per-agent witness facts non-authoritatively, rooted back to PostgreSQL source vectors.
work_items.schema: SHAKE256 witness chains linking every fact/edit to a source vector; causal-edge audit links; append-only proof-ledger rows (blueprint Anti-Bluffing: 'Cryptographic Witness Chains (SHAKE256)'; the current proof_records/proof_ledger.jsonl becomes DB-native under POSTGRES-003).
work_items.retention_tier: Warm + cold, append-only immutable ledger semantics.
work_items.encryption_boundary: Cryptographic integrity via SHAKE256 at rest; append-only with no in-place edit; contents are hashes and source-vector links only — no private payload.
work_items.migration_path: The proof/provenance ledger becomes PostgreSQL-native (POSTGRES-003 'planning and control tables made database-native'); witness-chain roots are anchored in PostgreSQL source vectors.
work_items.backup: PostgreSQL PITR (the append-only chain is fully PITR-covered).
work_items.replay: Re-verify the chain from its genesis root and re-scan Causal Graph Verification; an entry with no historical root is deterministically rejected (blueprint anti-bluffing).
work_items.rollback: Append-only: a bad entry is superseded by a corrective appended entry, never deleted; the ledger has no destructive rollback (ledger invariant).
work_items.proof_task: POSTGRES-003 (planning and control tables made database-native, incl. provenance/proof tables) + the existing proof_ledger discipline.
work_items.decision: PostgreSQL anchors the embedded ledger; witness chains rooted in PostgreSQL source vectors make hallucinated state mathematically rejectable (blueprint SHAKE256 witness chains).

work_items.surface: Artifact content class (content-addressed source blobs + materialized / exported build trees)
work_items.owner: PostgreSQL is the source of truth: content-addressed source blobs are canonical in PostgreSQL via codedb_store_pg::PgStore under the BlobStore contract; materialized/exported trees are disposable derived projections produced by envctl / CodeDB materialization.
work_items.schema: SHA-256 content hash of file bytes as blob identity (identical across backends); module_path metadata plus a branch/version selector for materialized trees (ADOPT-001 BlobStore / SHA-256 identity / exact-byte materialization; STORE-001 class 5).
work_items.retention_tier: Canonical blobs warm + cold in PostgreSQL; materialized trees are hot / ephemeral filesystem projections that are disposable and regenerated on demand.
work_items.encryption_boundary: At-rest for canonical blobs; secret-output guards on materialized exports (ADOPT-001 'secret-output guards'); projections carry no secrets.
work_items.migration_path: The CodeDB source-blob store migrates redb-durable -> PostgreSQL-canonical via a green live crates/codedb_store_pg/tests/blobstore_parity.rs run against a disposable PostgreSQL, one verified swap at a time under one preserved SHA-256 identity (STORE-001 correction; POSTGRES-001-F).
work_items.backup: PITR for canonical blobs; materialized trees need no backup (deterministically re-projectable).
work_items.replay: Exact-byte re-materialization from PostgreSQL rows via module_path metadata (deterministic; ADOPT-001 exact-byte materialization).
work_items.rollback: Re-project a prior branch/version; canonical blobs recover via PITR.
work_items.proof_task: ADOPT-001 (BlobStore / PgStore / SHA-256 / exact-byte materialization) + PGAUTH-004 (derived projection authority) + POSTGRES-008/POSTGRES-009 (envctl materialization + portable projected build proven end to end).
work_items.tier_residency: warm + cold (canonical blobs); hot / ephemeral (materialized trees)
work_items.decision: Canonical source blobs are owned by PostgreSQL under the SHA-256 parity contract; materialized trees are disposable one-way projections that never back-write canonical state (STORE-001 assignment 5 + correction; PGAUTH-004 invariants).

work_items.surface: Identity content class (user and agent identities, account records, permissions subject keys)
work_items.owner: PostgreSQL owns the canonical LifeOS identity records; external identity providers remain authoritative for their own credentials and are referenced, never copied.
work_items.schema: Stable user_id / agent_id primary identities, account records, and identity relations (Architecture Context Matrix 'Standard SQL metadata (e.g., user_id)'; POSTGRES-002 identity model).
work_items.encryption_boundary: At-rest plus role-based access; PII minimization — LifeOS stores identity references and account metadata, not raw external credentials (external credentials stay in the external-secret class L).
work_items.migration_path: Identity records ingest one-way into the PostgreSQL accounts/identity model (POSTGRES-002); external identity is referenced through the external-source contract (PGAUTH-005), never captured.
work_items.proof_task: POSTGRES-002 (relational graph and identity model) + PGAUTH-005 (external source authority; identity referenced not copied).
work_items.decision: PostgreSQL owns LifeOS identity exactly once; external identity providers are referenced under the external-source contract (STORE-001 assignments 1 + 6).

work_items.surface: Provenance content class (source commit/PR lineage, SHA-256 content identity, attestation references)
work_items.owner: PostgreSQL records provenance/lineage references; committed source pins anchor external attestation authority.
work_items.schema: Source commit + PR lineage, SHA-256 content identity, attestation-bundle references, adopted-capability provenance (STORE-001 invariant 'Provenance preserved'; ADOPT-001 every row carries source commit/PR; generated/adopted_capabilities.source.csv).
work_items.encryption_boundary: At-rest; references and hashes only, no raw secrets; attestation bundles are referenced, not inlined.
work_items.migration_path: Provenance travels WITH each migrated class: SHA-256 identity and commit/PR lineage are preserved on every cutover (STORE-001 invariant 5 'every migrated class keeps its SHA-256 identity and its source commit/PR lineage').
work_items.backup: PostgreSQL PITR plus committed git source pins (dual-anchored).
work_items.replay: Re-derive lineage from the commit graph and the SHA-256 content identities.
work_items.proof_task: ADOPT-001 (source commit/PR + provenance from recovery_adoption_map) + POSTGRES-003 (database-native provenance/proof tables).
work_items.decision: PostgreSQL holds provenance references while committed pins anchor external attestation; provenance is preserved across every verified cutover, never lost (STORE-001 invariant 5).

work_items.surface: Per-agent cognition content class (MicroLoRA adapters, SONA policy matrices, RL weights, FastGRNN routing) — enumerated for completeness per STORE-001 and PGAUTH-003
work_items.owner: AgentDB (.rvf) per agent — canonical only for one agent's cognition, non-authoritative for shared macro-state.
work_items.schema: .rvf format keyed by agent_id; MicroLoRA (Rank 1/2) adapter deltas, SONA policy matrices, reinforcement-learning weights (Thompson Sampling, Q-Learning, PPO), FastGRNN routing graph (blueprint section 2-3; Architecture Context Matrix AgentDB row).
work_items.retention_tier: Warm, edge-local per-agent .rvf file; not resident in the shared hot/warm/cold PostgreSQL tiers as authority.
work_items.encryption_boundary: Per-agent .rvf file boundary; witness-bound via SHAKE256 to PostgreSQL source vectors; no shared-secret exposure.
work_items.migration_path: None — AgentDB is the intended owner of active cognition; shared knowledge promotes one-way AgentDB -> PostgreSQL knowledge graph, with no back-write to per-agent cognition (STORE-001 class 4; PGAUTH-003).
work_items.backup: The .rvf file itself; regenerable from the frozen base model plus the agent's training history (STORE-001 class 4 recovery).
work_items.replay: Replay the training history against the base model to reconstruct adapters and policy matrices.
work_items.rollback: Restore a prior .rvf snapshot; COW branching over cognition commits the winning timeline (Architecture Context Matrix AgentDB COW).
work_items.proof_task: PGAUTH-003 (per-agent cognition authority contract bounded) + POSTGRES-006 (redb and AgentDB runtime boundaries defined).
work_items.tier_residency: edge-local (.rvf); one-way promotion into warm PostgreSQL knowledge graph
work_items.decision: AgentDB owns per-agent cognition exactly once and cannot silently own shared user-space truth (STORE-001 assignment 4; PGAUTH-003 invariant).

work_items.surface: External-secret / credential authority content class (API keys, attestation bundles, source pins) — required to satisfy the gate's encryption-boundary and no-private-data clauses
work_items.owner: OS keyring + committed source pins (external authority owns the secret value); LifeOS holds only a local reference.
work_items.schema: Local reference key + keyring service/account identity (never the secret value); mirrors the STORE-001 class 6 boundary and the envctl secrets-engine / secretd / secretctl broker (ADOPT-002).
work_items.retention_tier: n/a — secrets never enter the LifeOS hot, warm, or cold tiers; only references are retained.
work_items.encryption_boundary: THE hard boundary: raw secrets live only in the OS keyring; PostgreSQL stores references and provenance, never raw secrets; redb never holds secrets; envctl secretd/secretctl brokers access. This is the clause that operationalizes 'no private data is exposed'.
work_items.migration_path: None (STORE-001 class 6); the reference may be re-pointed but the secret is never versioned into a LifeOS tier.
work_items.backup: OS keyring + committed source pins (STORE-001 class 6 recovery).
work_items.replay: Fail-closed re-solicit / re-retrieve on outage (POSTGRES-001-E); the secret value is re-fetched from the keyring, never replayed from a LifeOS log.
work_items.rollback: n/a — the reference is re-pointed; the secret is never captured into LifeOS state to roll back.
work_items.proof_task: PGAUTH-005 (external source authority contract bounded) + ADOPT-002 (envctl secrets-engine / secretd / secretctl adopted).
work_items.tier_residency: reference-only; no LifeOS tier residency for the raw value
work_items.decision: External authorities own secrets; LifeOS references and never captures secret values, keeping every tier free of private data (STORE-001 assignment 6; PGAUTH-005).

global_invariants: One owner per data class: each of the 12 classes names exactly one canonical owner (redb for hot; PostgreSQL+RuVector for warm/relational/vector/graph; PostgreSQL PITR archive for cold; PostgreSQL for embedded-ledger/artifact/identity/provenance anchors; AgentDB for per-agent cognition; OS keyring for secrets). PostgreSQL owning several content classes does not violate single-ownership: no class has two owners.
Single durable writer: PostgreSQL owns every durable macro-state class exactly once; redb never becomes a second durable writer for a PostgreSQL-owned class (STORE-001 invariant 1).
Forward-only sync: redb -> envctl -> PostgreSQL; AgentDB -> PostgreSQL knowledge graph; PostgreSQL -> envctl materialize -> filesystem. No undirected dual-write on any edge (STORE-001 invariant 2).
Verified cutover: a class leaves redb-durable only after a green live blobstore_parity.rs run against a disposable PostgreSQL for that class; never by assumption (STORE-001 invariant 3).
No sidecar: RuVector runs as a native PostgreSQL extension; there is no separate vector database in the target (STORE-001 invariant 4).
Provenance preserved: every migrated class keeps its SHA-256 identity and its source commit/PR lineage (STORE-001 invariant 5).

No private data exposed: raw secrets live only in the OS keyring (class L); every other class stores references, hashes, or derived content, never raw secret values; this planning artifact contains no credentials, keys, tokens, or user PII.
completion_gate: Every data class has one owner, schema, retention tier, encryption boundary, migration path, backup, replay, rollback, and proof task; no private data is exposed.
downstream: INTEL-002 (local intelligence compute and mesh substrate plan) and INTEL-003 (regenerative configuration, secrets, and learning plan) build on this governed data contract; PGAUTH-006 reconciles the same ownership assignments into the closed synchronization-and-recovery matrix.
